cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem step by step. First, the question is about Jane's birthday and calculating a date a month ago from today. Let me read the problem again carefully. Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Today is her 16-year-old birthday. What is the date a month ago in MM/DD/YYYY? The options are given, so I need to figure out which one is correct. First, I need to determine today's date. Jane was born on the last day of February 2001. Since February typically has 28 days, except in leap years when it has 29. 2001 is not a leap year because leap years are divisible by 4, and 2001 isn't. So the last day of February 2001 would be February 28, 2001. Now, today is her 16th birthday. That means today's date is February 28, 2017 (because 2001 + 16 = 2017). Wait, but wait a second. Let me check if 2016 was a leap year. Yes, 2016 is divisible by 4, so it's a leap year. But that doesn't affect her birthday unless she was born in a leap year. She was born in 2001, which is not a leap year, so her birthday is always on February 28. So today is February 28, 2017. The question is asking for the date a month ago. So if today is February 28, 2017, then a month ago would be January 28, 2017. Let me verify. If I subtract a month from February 28, 2017, I get January 28, 2017. Because February has 28 or 29 days, but when subtracting a month, you just go to the same day in the previous month. So 28th of February minus one month is 28th of January. Looking at the options, January 28, 2017 is option D. Let me double-check the other options to make sure I didn't make a mistake. Option A: 11/12/2016 – That would be November 12, 2016, which is over a year ago. Not a month ago. Option B: 01/21/2017 – January 21, 2017. That's 7 days before January 28, so not the correct date. Option C: 01/14/2017 – January 14, 2017. That's 14 days before January 28, so also not correct. Option D: 01/28/2017 – January 28, 2017. As discussed, this is one month before February 28, 2017. So this is correct. Option E: 02/03/2017 – February 3, 2017. That's 25 days after February 28, so not correct. Therefore, the correct answer is D. Wait, but let me think again. Sometimes when you subtract a month, depending on the day of the month, you might have to adjust if the previous month doesn't have that day.
